ogTitle: Cuthbert vs Daggar Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cuthbert and Daggar Hills.
heading: Cuthbert vs Daggar Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cuthbert, Western Australia and Daggar Hills, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Daggar Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Cuthbert (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cuthbert or Daggar Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cuthbert has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Daggar Hills scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cuthbert scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Daggar Hills scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cuthbert or Daggar Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Daggar Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.38% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
